_README: Prospera Energy LEGACY ALBERTA wellbores (BA A5BH): Brooks / Red Earth / Cleardale — the dossier §9 disposition candidates (renumbered from §11, 2026-06-09).

WHITE TUNDRA (BA A93Y: Loyalist Twp29 R14W4 + Provost Twp34 R7W4) EXCLUDED per Owen 2026-06-06: active Feb–Apr 2026 Loyalist reactivations + Provost parked as a restart queue = Prospera intends to HOLD the WTP assets; they are not plausible disposition inventory.
18 WI wellbores ever-reporting under A5BH in Petrinex AB Vol 2022-01..2026-04, swept by UWI (not operator) so wells reporting through other operators' batteries are caught.

This deck is a BUYER'S SCREEN of what Prospera would plausibly sell — it is NOT a Sayer/EAG package; no flyer or VDR exists. recommendation_ui:true re-enables Recommended tags + the Selected+Rec FA scope (retired template-wide otherwise).

asset.name: Prospera Energy — Legacy Alberta Package (Brooks / Red Earth / Cleardale)

presentation.well_overrides_force_note: 103/15-02 is a HORIZONTAL: ST37 licenses it to 2,884 m total in a ~1,100-1,300 m TVD field (offsets 1,121-1,243 m), Prospera notes call it a horizontal, and its 2024 licence + own Pekisko AAA pool fit the hz program. The GIS pull carries md 1,295 m and no lateral (wrong bore/event) — forced to the ST37 facts so the Type column reads Horizontal. 103/15-11 (Red Earth): MD 2,311 m vs TVD 1,339 m (1.7x), 2011 licence in the Red Earth Slave Point hz-drilling era — classed horizontal; confirm trajectory via Geoview layer 9 / AccuMap if it matters to a bid.

100113601816W400.restart_note: Prospera notes (2026-06-10): drilled & cased, never completed. Proposed: complete with acid/sand frac. Cost $200k. Claimed potential 62.9 bbl/d ($3,180/bbl/d). UNRISKED vendor numbers.

102053101815W400.restart_note: Prospera notes (2026-06-10): 'shut in Jan-2025' (never flowed to sales). Proposed: workover + proper acid stim. Cost $100k. Claimed potential 37.7 bbl/d ($2,650/bbl/d).

102161101916W400.restart_note: Prospera notes (2026-06-10): 'shut in Nov-2024' (never flowed to sales). Proposed: workover + proper acid stim. Cost $100k. Claimed potential 12.6 bbl/d ($7,949/bbl/d).

102160201916W402.restart_note: Prospera notes (2026-06-10): shut-in oil well (2003), next to the 15-2 horizontal. His verdict: 'Nothing'.

103150201916W400.restart_note: Prospera notes (authored Michael Blair, last modified 2025-10-14 — PRE-DATES the Feb-2026 failure), two line items: (1) toe acid stim on the then-PRODUCING horizontal, $175k, 22 -> 31.4 bbl/d claimed. (2) gas tie-in $150k (well flares its gas). NEITHER is a costed repair for the February mechanical failure — no such estimate exists. Our base NPV charges $175k as a service-rig PROXY for restoration; true ticket could run $50k (pump change) to $300k+ (fishing job). First diligence question.

102042001915W400.restart_note: Prospera notes (authored Michael Blair, 2025-10-14): pumping oil well, on production since 2012. 'Possible stim' — no cost quoted; no charge taken. WATER NOW CHARGED IN BASE at trucked third-party $4.01/bbl (95.6 bbl/d water, masked receiver, no in-package disposal at Brooks): NPV $185k, economic life 7.1y. A 16-02 injection conversion restores most of the ~$0.8M this cost removed. Nothing reported to Petrinex since Dec-2025.

102160802015W400.restart_note: Prospera notes (2026-06-10): pumping oil well, on production since 2009. 'Possible stim on well' — no cost or uplift quoted; no charge taken.

102083601816W400.restart_note: Prospera notes (2026-06-10): pumping oil well currently DOWN FOR WORKOVER. Proposed: workover. Cost $30k. Claimed restored rate 31.4 bbl/d ($954/bbl/d — his best capital-efficiency claim; trailing Petrinex rate was ~0.5 bbl/d, so treat with care).

100093601816W405.restart_note: Prospera notes (2026-06-10): shut in Feb-2025, needs workover. Cost $30k. Claimed potential 12.6 bbl/d ($2,385/bbl/d).
100093601816W405.rec_formation: U.Mann Z4Z + Bow Isl. gas (abzd; stranded)

100061708112W600.restart_note: Prospera notes (2026-06-10): pumping. Proposed: clean up wellbore + stimulate with acid (WRXSOL-ECO blend). Cost $12k. Claimed 3.1 bbl/d.

100141708112W600.restart_note: Prospera notes (2026-06-10): pumping. Proposed: clean up wellbore + stimulate with acid (WRXSOL-ECO blend). Cost $12k. Claimed 3.1 bbl/d.

100161808112W600.restart_note: Prospera notes (2026-06-10): pumping. Proposed: clean up wellbore + stimulate with acid (WRXSOL-ECO blend). Cost $15k. Claimed 6.3 bbl/d.

combined_audit.d011_aro_by_uwi_note: Abandoned-NOT-reclaimed Grain/Alsask bores: wellbore abandonment done, surface reclamation outstanding. The status parser scores 'Abandoned' as fully closed ($0); override to D011 reclamation-only ($27,250/bore, Aug-2025 schedule) until reclamation certificates are confirmed on the AER licensee statement.
combined_audit.abandon_note: Per-well NPVs charge the flat abandonment slider per wellbore at cutoff + delay, floored at shut-in value. TWO LIABILITY BASES: full A5BH licence book $1.96MM (18 Petrinex bores + 4 D&C + 1 suspended at ~$80k, + 7 abandoned-not-reclaimed Grain/Alsask at ~$27k reclamation) vs producing-asset carve-out $1.45MM (18 bores only). Inheriting the abandoned-unreclaimed bores is a DEAL-STRUCTURE CHOICE, not a given — the $0.5MM gap is a bid lever. Replace with the AER licensee liability statement / calculated D011 schedule before any real bid.
combined_audit.startup_capex_note: FA startup default $409k = the restart/workover capex the DCA runner charges per-well at t=0 (Prospera notes' costs where priced: 15-02 $175k; three unpriced suspended Red Earth bores at $50k default; 09-36/5 + 08-36 $30k; two Pouce Coupe stims $12k). Keeps the FA package NPV consistent with the Well View sum. CAVEAT: slider is package-level — if you scope the FA to a well subset, rescale it. Licence-transfer/diligence costs are NOT in this number.
combined_audit.notes: PROSPERA LEGACY-AB DISPOSITION SCREEN — CONFIRM BEFORE TRUSTING ANY NPV: (1) 18 wellbores = every WI bore ever reporting under BA A5BH in AB Petrinex 2022-01..2026-04, swept by UWI so bores reporting through third-party batteries are included. WHITE TUNDRA (A93Y, Loyalist+Provost) EXCLUDED — active 2026 reactivations say Prospera is holding those (Owen 2026-06-06). (2) NOT a marketed package — this is OUR guess at what they'd sell (dossier §9); no flyer NPV to reconcile against. The leftover 'Sayer 2P' line in the DCA runner output is vestigial. (3) Oil deck = AB MEDIUM (MSW-linked, ~$92-96 CAD realized at $75 WTI / -$5 diff), NOT Luseland's WCS heavy deck. Brooks legacy density ASSUMED medium — CONFIRM from AB ST37/AccuMap; Red Earth (Slave Pt/Granite Wash) may be lighter. (4) wcs_diff -5 placeholder (light/medium AB realization), same convention as Decimus. (5) var_water_opex $1.00 assumes own-IF disposal — Red Earth IF (ABIF0148399) is in-package; Brooks routing unconfirmed, may truck ($4). (6) Royalty 12% blended AB Crown ARF/MRF — Decimus-measured Crown rates ran 6-9%. (7) 11 of 18 bores are Suspended Oil — and these are INDIVIDUAL deaths (staggered shut-in dates 2024-04..2026-02), NOT a corporate batch shut-in (Owen 2026-06-06). Each well is down for a well-specific reason — most plausibly mechanical failures Prospera chose not to fix while its capital went to SK/Loyalist reactivations. ADVERSE SELECTION APPLIES: Prospera restarts wells for a living at ~$81k/well; if THEY won't fund these restarts, revealed preference says these sit below their hurdle (costlier workovers, worse expected rates, or remote one-off mobilization at Red Earth/Cleardale vs campaign economics in their SK backyard). The 0.82x blended restart median is the problem-well class — apply it WITH workover capex ($50-150k incl. service-rig mobilization, not $30-80k turn-on) and a material never-comes-back probability. Restorable ~29 bbl/d is a CEILING, not an estimate. Natural buyer = an operator already in the area whose marginal mobilization cost is near zero. (8) deemed_liability $1.45MM = 18 bores x ~$80k placeholder incl. SCVF/GM adders folded by the build; aro_mode flat-per-well until a real D011 depth schedule is wired. (9) Status mix at build: 5 Oil, 11 Suspended Oil, 1 Disposal, 1 Suspended; producing rule = oil in 2026-03/04. (10) Three areas -> three stamped maps. KEY WELL: 103/15-02-019-16W4 (Brooks) = over half of package NPV and DARK since early Feb-2026 (died mid-month at ~25 bbl/d after 17 clean months — mechanical signature). If it stays down the package is worth roughly half the headline. Single biggest diligence item.
combined_audit.ngl_note: No NGL stream modeled — single-well oil batteries; any solution gas is conserved/fuel or vented per Petrinex, no NGL reporting found for these bores.
combined_audit.water_routing_note: BROOKS + POUCE COUPE WATER NOW PRICED AS ROUTED (runner change 2026-06-10): trucked third-party ~$4.01/bbl (Petrinex dispositions show 97-100% to masked commercial receivers; only in-package disposal is Red Earth 07-11, which keeps own-IF ~$0.41/bbl, 93% in-package). Effect: 04-20 $996k -> $185k (economic life 25.8y -> 7.1y), 09-36/5 flips to leave-dead, package $4.36M -> $3.48M, 15-02 now 71% of value. UPSIDE: converting 16-02 to injection/disposal claws most of this back (~$0.8M). NOTE: the FA tab's var_water_opex slider stays $1.00 (correct for Red Earth) — the FA therefore OVERSTATES Brooks/PC wells vs Well View; set the slider to 4 when scoping FA to Brooks.
combined_audit.recomp_economics_note: Recomp NPVs are SCREEN-GRADE synthetics on the deck's own economics: restored rate 2.5 bbl/d oil + 8 bbl/d water (in-package Granite Wash/Slave Point producer analogs), 8%/yr eff decline, deck price/opex/royalty, minus recompletion cost ($125k zone-change at 08-11, $100k reactivation at 01-11). UNRISKED after-cost; at 50% mechanical/zone risk 08-11 goes ~-$20k and 01-11 ~breakeven — these are rig-already-on-site options, not value drivers. 09-36's abandoned uphole gas zones carry $0 (no gas egress at the SWB; pure optionality if the 15-02 gas tie-in ever happens). Rights coverage unverified (Geoview layer 31 / VDR).

extra_tabs.label: Deal Summary
extra_tabs.html:
Prospera Energy — Legacy Alberta Package

Brooks/Countess · Red Earth · Pouce Coupe · (+ Grain/Alsask liability bores)
RECOMMENDED: buy Brooks/Countess only — base NPV₁₀ ≈ $2.4M at flat $65 WTI · $652k closure book Brooks: a stalled 2024 Pekisko program — the 15-02 horizontal + 2 legacy producers + 1 drilled-&-cased vertical (11-36) awaiting completion · plus 2 watered-out re-entries we do not recommend Brooks upside modifications: 16-02 injection conversion ends the trucked-water cost (~$0.8M back) · ~44 mcf/d of flared gas runs a mining container
Overview Value & sensitivities The 2024 Pekisko program Mining upside Upside ledger Risk register Open diligence
Section 1

Overview

  • Prospera non-core assets. The legacy Alberta position, plausibly for sale while Prospera's capital goes to its Saskatchewan CHOPS core.

The four areas at a glance

AreaNotesNPV₁₀ · flat $65
BROOKS / COUNTESS — 9 bores (BUY)The 2024 Pekisko program (15-02 hz + 1 drilled-&-cased, 11-36, + 2 re-entered verticals) + two legacy producers · single-well batteries · 15-02 flares its gas · water trucked third-party · the recommended purchase$2.4M
RED EARTH — 10 boresGranite Wash Q4Q + one Slave Point horizontal · own gathering + disposal · net production value ≈ its $751k closure book — roughly washes; take only if bundled cheaply$0.11M
POUCE COUPE — 3 boresBoundary C (Triassic) · deep isolated singles 700 km from Brooks · value ≈ closure — pass$0.06M
GRAIN / ALSASK — 7 boresAbandoned 1999–2021, not reclamation-certified · invisible to any volumetrics-only sweep · pure liability (~$27k reclamation each) — refuse unless paid−$0.13M

Brooks well map

Interactive — drag to pan, click a well for its detail, toggle Streets/Satellite (top-right). Dots are the 9 Brooks wells on their AER-exact surface locations; the dashed grey line is 15-02’s horizontal lateral. The pink dashed blocks are CNRL/Astara Crown PNG agreements in adjacent sections — they do not sit over the Prospera wells, which fall in the gaps. A point query of the Crown layer at each well returns no Crown agreement, so the well sections are most likely freehold (mineral ownership not public — confirm in the VDR).

Current production: ~2 bbl/d
only 16-08 running (avg Jan–Mar 2026); 15-02 offline since February
Workover / restart capex ~$385k → adds ~64 bbl/d
restart 15-02 (repair + toe completion + gas tie-in, ~45 bbl/d), 04-20, 09-36, 08-36 (our forecast). Excludes the two non-recommended verticals.
Completion capex ~$200k → adds ~10 bbl/d
11-36, the one never-completed (drilled-&-cased) well, at ~10 bbl/d (peer-based, our forecast)

Brooks wells

Well (type · year · zone · product)Now (avg Jan–Mar 2026)Our forecastStatus

Prospera's notes: heel half completed with a blank liner over good pay shows (not open); toe open-hole and producing; flaring all gas, can tie into a gas line. Our read: it produces from ~half the lateral — opening the heel + a gas tie-in are the upside.

Workover · $30k · Prospera claim ~31 bbl/d. Re-entered legacy CNRL bore (1998 — our finding). Prospera's notes: pumping oil well, down with broken rods. Our read: cheap mechanical fix, but it flushed 179→1 bbl/d, so sustained rate is low.
11-36 · Vt · 2024 · Pekisko · drilled & cased00~10—Never completed
Completion · $200k · Prospera claim ~63 bbl/d. Greenfield, no legacy bore (our finding). Prospera's notes: drilled and cased but never completed or equipped; proposed work — complete with acid/sand frac.
05-31 · Vt · 2024 · Pekisko YY · oil (shut-in)00——Shut in Jan-25 · not recommended
Prospera's proposed work: acid/sand frac stim ($100k), claim ~38 bbl/d. Re-entered CNRL bore; produced only ~4 months (Oct-24→Jan-25) to ~6 bbl/d, then watered out to 96% and was shut in. We do not recommend the stim — fracking a 95%+-water well risks more water, not oil.
16-11 · Vt · 2024 · Pekisko UND · oil (shut-in)00——Shut in Nov-24 · not recommended
Prospera's proposed work: acid/sand frac stim ($100k), claim ~13 bbl/d. Re-entered CNRL bore; produced only ~4 months (Aug→Nov-24) to ~6.5 bbl/d, then watered out to 99% and was shut in. We do not recommend the stim — fracking a 99%-water well risks more water, not oil.

Disposal-conversion candidate (our idea; not a producer). Prospera's notes: shut-in oil well next to 15-02; their proposed work — “Nothing.”
Brooks total~2~0~76~56

† = Prospera's claimed post-workover rate, which our model does not support (these are the never-completed verticals and the down well — see Section 3). Unmarked post-workover figures are our own restored-rate projection.

The formations — and why Pekisko matters

  • Brooks = Pekisko carbonate. A Mississippian limestone that responds to acid stimulation.
  • Horizontal peer check (48 wells, first oil 2022+). 15-02 underperforms the horizontal peers — most likely because its completion is unfinished (only the open-hole toe is on; the heel sits behind a blank liner over good pay). (See Section 3 chart.)
  • Vertical peer check (272 wells producing 2022–26). The two re-entered wells (05-31, 16-11) each produced only ~4 months to ~6 bbl/d before watering out to 96–99% and being shut in — so we do not recommend restarting them (a stim on a 95%+-water well risks more water than oil). Only 11-36 is a true never-completed well — no history, carried at a peer ~10 bbl/d as an unproven option only. (See Section 3 chart.)

Two upside modifications, costed

  • 16-02 → water-injection/disposal conversion. Cost ~$150–300k (regulatory approval + integrity test). Value ~$0.8M (NPV₁₀): ends the trucked third-party water cost now dragging 04-20 and the suspended wells, and adds pressure support to the Pekisko cluster.
  • Flared gas → on-site mining. The ~44 mcf/d 15-02 flares can run ~150 kW of on-site generation (vs $0 from flaring). Two coins, same genset + container:
    • Bitcoin: ~$200k all-in (used genset+hookup ~$115k, container ~$45k, ~48 used miners ~$43k) → ~$140k/yr gross. Low risk, deep resale.
    • Zcash: ~$350–660k all-in (same infra + ~51 Antminer Z15 Pro at $3.7k list to $7–10k secondary) → ~$700k/yr gross — ~5× Bitcoin per kW today, but high risk (ZEC −50% in 48h this month; sold-out hardware).
    Equipment redeploys after the gas fades; beats Prospera's $150k pipeline tie-in. Live toggle + economics in Section 4.
Section 2

Value & sensitivities

Key modelling parameters

ParameterValueBasis
Oil price$82 CAD/bbl, flat (no escalation)Flat US$65 WTI base case — Mixed Sweet benchmark US$65 less a US$5 quality differential, ÷ 0.73 USD/CAD = $82.2 CAD/bbl held flat for life, no escalation. Bear / bull are flat US$50 / US$80 ($61.6 / $102.7 CAD) — see the price-scenario table. We assume Brooks crude is medium gravity (consistent with the Pekisko); if it actually sells as heavy oil, revenue drops ~25–30%.
DeclinePer-well Arps, floored 8%/yr eff (oil)Each well's decline is fitted to its own history, but we never let a forecast decline slower than 8%/yr. That minimum is what's actually driving the big wells — 15-02's own 17 months of data fit at 5.9%, so it's forecast at the 8% minimum for 30 years. The single biggest valuation lever in the package.
Restart capexProspera's own per-well cost where they gave one ($12–200k); $50k assumed where they didn'tThe cost to bring a shut-in well back is subtracted up-front inside that well's value. If restarting can't beat simply leaving the well shut-in and abandoning it later, we hold the well at that "do nothing" value — about −$7,400, which is the small present cost of its eventual abandonment — rather than forcing a restart that loses money. The interactive screen's startup-cost slider defaults to the same total ($409k across all wells) so both views agree.
Opex / royalty$1,500/well/mo + $3/bbl; 12% royaltySingle-well battery convention; Crown/freehold blend (measured Crown rates ran 6–9% on Decimus — 12% is conservative).
Water handlingTrucked third-party (~$4.01/bbl) — Brooks has no water lineProvincial disposition records show 97–100% of Brooks water going to a third-party commercial disposer (identity masked in public data) from batteries with no water line — hence trucked rates. Converting 16-02 to injection (Section 6) is the lever that puts most of this cost back.
Closure~$0.8M across the 9 Brooks bores + 5 batteries$70.6k standard per bore, $87k for the 15-02 horizontal, plus ~$150k surface for the 5 single-well batteries. Full breakdown in the risk register; placeholder until the AER licensee statement.

Sensitivity tornado — what moves Brooks value

Operational and price swings in Brooks NPV₁₀ around the $2.37M base (existing producers + 15-02 completed). 15-02 completion dominates the downside — if it fails the base falls to ~−$0.3M. Flat WTI $50 / $80 swings it $1.4M–$3.4M. Heavy-oil pricing (if Brooks sells heavy) is the other downside. The Pekisko verticals are excluded — a non-recommended option, not part of the base.

Brooks production profile

Forecast oil + gas rate for the recommended Brooks base — existing producers plus 15-02 fully completed, verticals excluded. Oil peaks ~58 bbl/d (15-02's toe + heel) declining on the 8%/yr floor; associated gas ~52 mcf/d. Flat $65 WTI.

Deal cash flow — flat $65 WTI (workovers and completions included)

Price scenarios — flat decks, no escalation

ScenarioWTI (flat)Realized heavy (CAD/bbl)Brooks NPV₁₀
Bear$50$61.6$1.40M
Base$65$82.2$2.37M
Bull$80$102.7$3.44M

Every case is a flat price held for life — no escalation. Realized = (WTI − $5 quality differential) ÷ 0.73 FX. The same flat deck drives the FA portfolio, the cash-flow chart, and the per-well NPVs. Bear/base/bull span $1.40M–$3.44M on the base (existing producers + 15-02). The verticals add upside on top of this, staged and risked — they are not in the base.

Section 3

The 2024 Pekisko program — the vendor's headline, our risking

Brooks is not legacy scraps. ST37 shows Prospera brought four new Countess wells into the Pekisko in 2023–24 — the 15-02 horizontal (TD 2,884 m) plus three vertical follow-ups. Two of those verticals, 05-31 and 16-11, are re-entries of existing CNRL wellbores — Prospera took over the old CNRL bores and deepened/re-completed them down to the Pekisko (~1,200–1,340 m) instead of drilling new holes. 11-36 is the exception — a greenfield well, and 15-02 (the horizontal) likewise sits on a CNRL surface location. So the program re-used CNRL's existing shallow wellbores and surface access, going deeper to the Pekisko. (The mineral-rights ownership at these freehold sections isn't public — see the corrected rights note in Section 1.) Then capital pivoted to Saskatchewan and the program stalled.

Well — Prospera's proposed workTheir costTheir claimed rateOur projected rateOur value (NPV₁₀)
11-36 — complete the cased hole (acid/sand frac)$200k62.9 bbl/d~10 bbl/d~$0.4M
05-31 — workover + acid stim (not recommended)$100k37.7 bbl/d— watered out−$7,371
16-11 — workover + acid stim (not recommended)$100k12.6 bbl/d— watered out−$7,371
15-02 — repair + toe acid stim + open the heel (full completion)
$325k all-in (Prospera's estimate): $175k to complete the toe + $150k restart/tie-in — the heel is already completed and pumping, so there is no separate heel job		$175k31.4 bbl/d~45 bbl/d$2.96M
08-36 — workover$30k31.4 bbl/d~5 bbl/d$0 — restart doesn't pay
Program total$605k~177 bbl/d~60 bbl/d~$3.3M

Our projected rates and NPVs are built from the public production fleet (charts below). For 11-36 — the one true drilled-&-cased vertical (never produced) — we apply a fresh-vertical IP of ~10 bbl/d, the upper-middle of 272 vertical Pekisko/Banff oil wells now producing (median ~5 bbl/d), not the 12.6–62.9 bbl/d Prospera claims; it is worth ~$0.4M risked net of completion capex. The two re-entries (05-31, 16-11) already produced and watered out to 96–99%, so we value them on that history and do not recommend a restart. 15-02 is valued on its own measured history. 08-36 already flushed and died, so a restart doesn't pay.

How real carbonate wells perform — does the rock support these rates?

Horizontal (48 peers, first oil 2022+). Real Banff/Pekisko horizontals peak ~120 bbl/d then decline steeply (P50 → ~42 by month 12). 15-02's solid line is its real history — but per the CEO that came from only the open-hole toe (~half the lateral); the heel sits behind a blank liner over good pay. We don't rewrite that history. The forecast opens the heel now, and treats the go-forward as two pieces: the flat mature toe (justified by 15-02's own 14-month plateau) plus a fresh heel that declines steeply early like the cohort — combined, ~48 bbl/d falling to ~27 over two years before flattening, a P50–P75 peer well. Needs the Feb-26 repair plus the heel-completion capex. The toe-only base (17 bbl/d) is our conservative NPV anchor; the gap between the two lines is what opening the heel buys.

Vertical (272 wells producing 2022–26). No 2022+ vertical carbonate wells were drilled — the modern play is horizontal-only — so this is the live fleet, not a normalized type curve. Vertical Pekisko/Banff strippers run a median ~5 bbl/d declining ~11%/yr, with a tail of Banff verticals holding 20–40 bbl/d nearly flat. Our ~10 bbl/d / ~10%/yr D&C assumption (red star) sits above the median rate at a typical decline — reasonable, mildly optimistic on rate.

All Prospera line items are Blair's October-2025 plans, pre-dating 15-02's February failure — none is a costed repair for it.

Cut both ways and the negotiating zone writes itself: Prospera will anchor on $30M of potential; the data supports paying for the existing production (~$3.5–5M with 15-02 fully completed) plus modest option value on three cased holes.

Section 4

Stranded gas → power (mining) upside

Brooks has gas with nowhere to go: 15-02 was flaring ~44 mcf/d while producing (Prospera's own notes; corroborated by the provincial gas volumes), the single-well batteries have no sales line, and Prospera's proposed fix is a $150k pipeline tie-in. Gas being burned for nothing is the textbook case for on-site generation. The gas supports ~150 kW of generation at the 15-02 pad; what you plug into those generators is a choice between Bitcoin and Zcash. All figures at June-2026 economics; both fade with the gas (forecast total: 20.2 MMcf at ~55%/yr decline → a 2–3 year play with portable equipment).

Bitcoin — bankable
Capex ~$200k all-in (used gas genset + hookup ~$115k · container ~$45k · ~48 used S19-class miners ~$43k). Revenue ~$140k/yr at full power. Low risk; genset + miners resell anywhere.
Zcash — aggressive
Capex $350–660k (same genset + container + ~51 Antminer Z15 Pro). Revenue ~$700k/yr at full power — ~5× Bitcoin per kW today, but high risk (ZEC −50% in 48 h this month; sold-out hardware).
CoinCapex (all-in)PaybackRev Yr1Yr2Yr3Yr4
Bitcoin~$200k~gas window — mining revenue (~$168k/4yr) is < capex, so equipment resale closes it~$96k~$43k~$20k~$9k
Zcash$350–660k<1 yr at low capex if ZEC holds (~1.4 yr at high) — price risk dominates~$482k~$217k~$98k~$44k

Revenue is gross, declining with the gas (~55%/yr off 44 mcf/d → 20.1 MMcf life); net of generator O&M and labour runs lower. Both coins share the same genset + container, so the choice is reversible. Either beats Prospera's $150k fixed pipeline tie-in, which can't pay back on 2–3 years of declining volumes. Zcash hardware lead time (Antminer Z15 Pro): ~2–6 weeks if you pay the SPOT/secondary premium, or 1–3 months waiting for a cheaper Bitmain batch — and with ZEC down ~50% in 48 h recently, the revenue premium can compress before the miners even land.

Section 5

Upside ledger — priced as options, never as the meal

"Value if it works" is a screening-level figure — a rough, order-of-magnitude estimate of the dollars each option adds (incremental NPV, or cost avoided) if it succeeds, before any diligence. Not booked value; a way to rank the options.

OptionValue if it works (rough)What unlocks it
Pekisko vertical 11-36 — complete the one cased D&C hole+~$0.4M
range $0.3–0.8M		Our peer-based forecast, net of 11-36's $200k completion (Prospera's quoted cost): a carbonate-vertical type curve (IP ~10 bbl/d, anchored to the 123-well public Pekisko vertical fleet, whose median is ~5 bbl/d) declining to a long stripper tail. 05-31 & 16-11 are excluded — they watered out to 96–99% and we do not recommend a restart. Far below Prospera's ~$17.5M claim, because a vertical contacts a fraction of what the 15-02 horizontal does — their 63 bbl/d-per-vertical claim is implausible. Realizing it needs the ~$400k spent and the rock cooperating.
16-02 injector/disposal conversion+~$0.8M
trucked-water cost avoided		16-02 — the well Prospera's own sheet calls worthless — happens to penetrate the Pekisko right beside 15-02. Converting it to a water-injection/disposal well would eliminate the trucked third-party disposal fees now charged in our base (the bulk of 04-20's lost value) and provide pressure support to the cluster of wells around it. A field-wide waterflood doesn't work (five separate pools), but this one-section scheme is worth a study. Main risk: in acid-stimulated carbonate, injected water tends to channel straight to producers. Pool continuity and pressure data are data-room questions.
Section 6

Risk register — Brooks purchase

The risks that move the Brooks decision, scored on our read of the public data. "Likelihood" is the chance the risk bites; "impact" is its effect on Brooks value if it does.

RiskLikelihoodImpactMitigation / diligence
15-02 offline since Feb-2026 (mechanical failure) — the single well behind most of Brooks valueOccurredHighPay for 15-02 only when it pumps (contingent on ~90 days post-repair); get the failure diagnosis and downhole inspection in the VDR.
Vertical forecast is peer-based, not history-supported — 11-36 carries ~$0.8M unrisked in the FA on a ~10 bbl/d peer forecast; 05-31 & 16-11 are carried at do-nothing (not forecast)Med-HighHigh05-31 & 16-11 watered out to 96–99% and are not forecast (do-nothing, −$7,371). Only 11-36 (never produced) carries the peer ~10 bbl/d — an assumption, risked to ~$0.4M in the per-well table. Underwrite the verticals as option value, not base; complete one and confirm rate before funding the others.
15-02 go-forward forecast — a two-part build (flat mature toe + fresh-heel decline), not a naive flat lineMedMedThe forecast models the open toe at its demonstrated flat rate plus a fresh-heel completion that declines steeply early like the cohort, then flattens — benchmarked to 48 real horizontals. Residual risk is the heel uplift (needs the repair + completion) and the tail length, not the shape. See the peer type curve.
Verticals — staged stim option; 05-31 & 16-11 carry water riskMedMedNever base. All three need a proper acid/sand frac (Prospera's note). 11-36 (virgin) is the clean test — complete it first (~$200k). 05-31 & 16-11 already watered out to 96–99%, so a frac risks more water than oil — stim them only if 11-36 prints and a water-source diagnosis clears them. Carry the three as a staged option, not base.
Mineral-rights ownership unconfirmed — no Crown agreement over the wells (likely freehold); who owns the rights and what depths Prospera leases isn't publicOpenHighConfirm freehold mineral ownership and Prospera's leased depths/zones in the VDR — this gates the purchase. Mining still counts only 15-02's own solution gas, so it doesn't depend on the shallow rights.
No own water disposal — trucked third-party rates drag opexConfirmedMedStudy the 16-02 injection/disposal conversion (also gives pressure support); confirm disposal contracts.
Abandonment / closure liability — full surface-inclusive ≈ $0.8M: 9 wells $652k D011 (abandonment + $27k/well reclamation) + 5 single-well batteries ≈$150k; no open SCVF/gas-migration, no orphansCertainLowPrice into the bid and confirm the AER licensee-liability transfer position (≈$1.0–1.3M on the regulatory/LMR deemed basis, the transfer gate). Low versus other Alberta acquisition options: these are recent (2003–2024), shallow (~1,200 m) wells with no orphan or deemed-problem sites and no open vent-flow — closure is only ~20–25% of asset value, where mature AB licence books routinely carry deemed liabilities that approach or exceed value (Prospera's own company-wide LLR is 1.09 — barely solvent). The liability-heavy slices — Grain/Alsask's 7 abandoned-not-reclaimed bores and Red Earth/Pouce Coupe where value ≈ closure — are exactly what a Brooks-only bid leaves behind.
Shared surface with CNRL — 7 of 9 Brooks wells sit on former CNRL pads (the four 2024 wells deep-twin CNRL bores; 04-20/16-08/16-02 are older TXNE-era wells on CNRL sites). Not all: 11-36 is greenfield, 09-36 is Prospera's own legacy bore.MedLow-MedConfirm surface-lease, road and access agreements convey for the shared sites; check no shared-facility entanglements.
Crude quality / price — medium-gravity differential and deckMedMedConfirm gravity and realized netback from production sales; run the price-deck sensitivity already in the model.
Section 7

Open diligence — the show-and-tell list

  1. 15-02 post-mortem. What exactly failed in February, what will the repair actually cost (no estimate exists — Prospera's $175k figure was a pre-failure stimulation plan), and what's the gas plan? Ask for the wellbore diagram and the February service report. This one answer moves 71% of the package.
  2. Pekisko continuity + pressure. DSTs/pressures for the sec-2 cluster (AccuMap); are AAA/UND/unpooled one accumulation? Confirms both the D&C case and the injector idea.
  3. Brooks crude density + marketing. Medium vs heavy decides ~$0.8M. One trucking ticket answers it.
  4. Water: who takes Brooks' water today, and at what fee? (The receiver is masked in public data.) Then price the 16-02 injection-well conversion against that fee — regulatory approval plus integrity testing runs ≈ $150–300k all-in.
  5. AER licensee liability statement + closure quota. Replace our $1.80M D011 placeholder; confirm reclamation status on Grain/Alsask (certificates or liability).
  6. Prospera's Pekisko mineral agreement. Confirm Prospera actually holds the Pekisko at these wells. A point query of the Crown agreement layer returns nothing over the wells — the sections are most likely freehold, so the rights and leased depths must come from the VDR. This gates the entire Brooks purchase.
  7. Mineral-rights coverage for the higher zones we'd recomplete, and any overriding royalties that would skim them.
  8. Structure probe. Asset carve-out vs full book vs corporate; whether WT is in scope. His openness here tells you how motivated the sale is.
Compiled 2026-06-10 from AER ST37 + Codecnv, Petrinex AB volumetrics & dispositions (2022-01→2026-04), Prospera's well sheet and White Tundra deck (rcvd 2026-06-10), and the per-well DCA/NPV build (prospera_ab_dca_npv.py · asset_config.prospera_ab.json). Every number traceable to source; vendor claims labeled as claims. Not investment advice.
